title: Lucia Ediage
diff_added:
'''Lucia Ediage Sona''' is a [[Cameroonian nationality law|Cameroonian]] public administrator serving as the Regional Delegate of the Ministry of Women's Empowerment and the Family (MINPROFF) for the [[Southwest Region (Cameroon)|South West Region]] of Cameroon.<ref>{{Cite web |title=At maiden women’s confab in Buea: Biaka University Institute schools women on leadership, empowerment, mentorship… |url=https://theguardianpostcameroon.com/post/6797/en/homeat-maiden-womens-confab-in-buea-biaka-university-institute-schools-women-on-leadership-empowerment-mentorship |access-date=2026-03-26 |website=At maiden women’s confab in Buea:  Biaka University Institute schools women on leadership, empowerment, mentorship… |language=en}}</ref> She is known for her involvement in initiatives promoting women's empowerment, digital inclusion, and the protection of girls’ rights in crisis-affected communities.<ref>{{Cite web |last=WANDJI |first=Paul Reinhard |date=2024-03-06 |title=Cameroon - 8 March Activities: Contest Launched To Reward Underprivileged Women |url=https://en.journalducameroun.com/cameroon-8-march-activities-contest-launched-to-reward-underprivileged-women/ |access-date=2026-03-26 |website=Journal du Cameroun |language=fr}}</ref>
Lucia Ediage Sona serves as the South West Regional Delegate of the Ministry of Women's Empowerment and the Family (MINPROFF), where she oversees programs aimed at improving the socio-economic conditions of women and families in the region.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Cameroun : Une Maison Digitale à Buea pour l'immersion numérique des femmes de la région du Sud-Ouest |url=https://www.camer.be/90355/11:1/cameroun-une-maison-digitale-a-buea-pour-limmersion-numerique-des-femmes-de-la-region-du-sud-ouest-cameroon.html |website=camer.be |date=2025-08-22 |access-date=2026-03-02}}</ref>
In August 2025, she participated in the inauguration of the Orange Digital Center “Maison Digitale” in [[Buea]], an initiative aimed at enhancing digital literacy and economic empowerment among women and girls.<ref name=":0" />
Speaking during the inauguration, Ediage Sona highlighted the importance of digital technologies in improving the visibility and economic participation of women's groups, noting that such initiatives align with national policies aimed at improving living conditions and fostering inclusion.<ref name=":0" />
The initiative forms part of broader efforts to equip women with skills in [[Information and communications technology|information and communication technologies]] (ICT), financial management, and entrepreneurship.<ref name=":0" />
In June 2025, she participated in the launch of the “Girls’ Vision for the Future Movement” in Buea, an initiative led by [[UNICEF]] and partners to amplify girls’ voices and promote their rights.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Amplifying voices of girls as change makers: UNICEF launches Girls’ Vision for the Future Movement in Buea |url=https://theguardianpostcameroon.com/post/5067/en/amp

title: Reminders of Him
diff_added: After a romantic date, Ledger insists on knowing how the evening of Scotty's death unfolded. Kenna then reads to him from her diary. It turns out that it was an accident through no fault of hers, and Kenna was able to free herself from the car. The blood supply to Scotty's arm had stopped, so Kenna assumed he was dead. Desperate, she sought help, but no one came to her aid. She was eventually arrested unconscious by the police. Overwhelmed with guilt over his death and the fact that she hadn't realized Scotty was still alive, she pleaded guilty. Only afterward did she learn that she was pregnant.

title: Site of Old Hannastown
diff_added:
On July 13, 1782, at 2 p.m., Hanna's Town was attacked and destroyed by a force of [[Seneca people|Seneca]] warriors accompanied by a small contingent of British soldiers, totaling between 100 to 300 people. Historians theorize that the Seneca warrior and diplomat [[Sayenqueraghta]] planned the attack, accompanied by Seneca chief [[Guyasuta]] and the [[British Indian Department]] captains John Powell and Robert Lottridge Jr. The attack was possibly in response to [[Daniel Brodhead]]'s invasion of [[Iroquois|Haudenosaunee (Iroquois)]] territory, which was supported by the Westmoreland County militia.{{sfn|Richardson|2007a|pp=21-23}}<ref name=":32">{{cite report |url=https://catalog.archives.gov/id/71995863 |title=National Register of Historic Places Registration: Pennsylvania SP Old Hannastown, Site of |author=William F. Porter |date=January 1970 |publisher=National Archives and Records Administration |access-date=February 21, 2026 |type=none}} ({{NationalArchivesNote}})</ref>
Residents of Hanna's Town took refuge in the stockade fort. Two residents died in the attack, one being 13-year-old Peggy Shaw, who was shot while rescuing a young boy. Several Seneca warriors were killed or wounded. Raiding parties also attacked Miller's Station, about 2.5 miles away, where a wedding was taking place. Among those in attendance were Robert Hanna's wife, Elizabeth, and their daughter, Jane, who were taken to Fort Niagara and later released.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Boucher |first=John Newton |url=http://archive.org/details/historyofwestmor01bouc |title=History of Westmoreland County, Pennsylvania |last2=Jordan |first2=John W. (John Woolf) |date=1906 |publisher=New York, Chicago, The Lewis Publishing Company |others=Allen County Public Library Genealogy Center |pages=184}}</ref>{{sfn|Richardson|2007a|pp=21-23}}

title: Farlowella odontotumulus
diff_added: '''''Farlowella odontotumulus''''' is a species of freshwater [[Actinopterygii|ray-finned fish]] belonging to the [[Family (biology)|family]] [[Loricariidae]], the suckermouth armored catfishes, and the [[subfamily]] [[Loricariinae]], the mailed catfishes.<ref name = "Cof genus"/> This catfish has been reported from Brazil, Ecuador and Venezuela.  In Ecuador it is found in the [[Aguarico River]] basin, the [[Type locality (biology)|type locality]], and the ony locality it has definitely been recorded from. In Venezuela it has been reported from the [[Mavaca River]] basin and, in Brazil, it has been reported from the [[Rio Bugre]] in [[Mato Grosso]]. The records from Brazil and Venezuela require confirmation.<ref name = iucn/> This species grows to a maximum [[standard length]] of {{cvt|17.5|cm}}.<ref name = Fishbase>{{FishBase|Farlowella|odontotumulus|month=November|year=2025}}</ref>

[[File:Český svět - 3.3.1905 - Růžena Svobodová.jpg|thumb|Svobodová in a 1905 publication]]

'''Růžena Svobodová''' (née '''Růžena Čápová'''; 10 July 1868 – 1 January 1920) was a Czech writer.
Růžena Čápová was born in [[Mikulovice (Znojmo District)|Mikulovice]], a market town in southern [[Moravia]], Austria-Hungary. Her family moved to [[Prague]] when she was a child. When she was 12, her father died, and the surviving widow and children moved out of the city, but Růžena was sent to schools in Prague.<ref name="Vlasta">Veronika Kábrtová, [http://vlasta.kafe.cz/clanky/pribehy/2015/10/9/rozervana-ruzena-svobodova-zivot-mezi-dvema-muzi/ "Rozervaná Růžena Svobodová: Život mezi dvěma muži"] ''Vlasta.cz'' (9 October 2015).</ref>
During [[World War I]], she signed the [[Manifesto of Czech writers]], and was active in organizing the charity Czech Heart, which provided food relief and rural foster homes for the children of Prague.<ref name="Vlasta" /><ref>{{cite web |title=Usilovala o emancipaci žen a odmítala intimní lásku. Osudové ženy: Růžena Svobodová|url=https://dvojka.rozhlas.cz/usilovala-o-emancipaci-zen-a-odmitala-intimni-lasku-osudove-zeny-ruzena-7452029|publisher=[[Czech Radio]]|date=8 July 2023|access-date=4 September 2025}}</ref>

title: Technicolor
diff_added: In 1966, the company created a division known as Vidtronics, which developed a system used in the late 1960s and early 1970s that allowed experimenting with video gear and videotape to make ads and feature-length motion pictures by transferring the videotape to film for final release and distribution.<ref name="Videotape">[https://www.provideocoalition.com/from-kinescopes-to-digital-cinema/ From Kinescopes to Digital Cinema]</ref> Films made with this process were the 1973 film ''[[Why (film)|Why]]'', the 1971 film ''The Resurrection of Zachary Wheeler'', and the most famous film using this process, [[Frank Zappa]]'s 1971 film ''[[200 Motels]]'', which was originally shot on [[2 inch quadruplex videotape]], and then transferred to film by Technicolor, being the first independent film originally to be shot on videotape and distributed theatrically in 35 mm.

title: Intercompany crossovers in comics
diff_added:
** ''Superman/Spider-Man'' (2026): A virus infected Brainiac teams with Dr. Octopus to transfer the virus into the minds of humans on Earth. Superman and Spider-Man team up to put a stop to it.
**** ''[[Lois Lane]]'' & ''[[Mary Jane Watson|Mary Jane]]'': While their respective husbands battle a Sentinel, Mary Jane and Lois engage in girl talk. Lois ultimately gives the X-Man Gambit some of her playing cards so Gambit can help Superman and Spider-Man finish off the giant robot.
**** ''[[Superboy-Prime]]'' & ''[[Alien Costume Saga|The Amazing Spider-Man]]'': Set in the 1980s, Superboy finds himself in the Marvel Universe and lures the black costume clad Spider-Man into another dimension so he can hand him over to an alternate version of the High Evolutionary. Ultimately Superboy changes his mind and teams up with Spider-Man.
**** ''[[Superboy#Clark Kent|Superboy]]'' & ''[[Spider-Man 2099]]'': Spider-Man 2099 heads to 2039 to stop the corporations LexCorp and Alchemax from merging. This merger will have major repercussions in the future. He encounters and then teams up with Superboy (from the 30th century) and Batman Beyond.
**** ''[[Jimmy Olsen|Superman's Pal Jimmy Olsen]]'' & ''[[Carnage (character)|Carnage]]'': Jimmy gets a job at the Daily Bugle and while trying to find Spider-Man to photograph, he encounters and is murdered by Carnage.
**** ''[[Jonathan and Martha Kent|Jonathan Kent]]'' & ''[[Uncle Ben|Ben Parker]]'': Superman and Spider-Man talk about their respective fathers and how they had a huge impact on their lives. They don't realize that their fathers met years before they were born, teaming up to save some children during a storm.
**** ''[[Daily Planet]]'' & ''[[Daily Bugle]]'': J. Jonah Jameson has a nationally televised debate with Lois Lane about the merits of opinion in the media.
**** ''[[Power Girl]]'' & ''[[Punisher]]'': When the Punisher makes his way to an underground club to hunt supervillians, he encounters and teams up with Power Girl, who was there on a blind date. After the bad guys are defeated, the two of them go out together.

title: Bihar Reorganisation Act, 2000
diff_added:
| long_title     = An Act to provide for the reorganisation of the existing State of Bihar and for matters connected therewith.

The '''Bihar Reorganisation Act, 2000''' was a law passed by the [[parliament of India]] in 2000. [[Loksabha]] and [[Rajya sabha]] passed the bill on 2 and 11 August respectively and on 15 November 2000, which enabled creation of [[Jharkhand]] State out of [[Bihar]].<ref name=a>{{cite book|title=The Unrest Axle: Ethno-social Movements in Eastern India edited by Gautam Kumar Bera|pages=60–61|isbn=9788183241458|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=9qrmTdshzKQC&q=Bihar+State+reorganization+bill&pg=PA60|last1=Bera|first1=Gautam Kumar|year=2008|publisher=Mittal Publications }}</ref><ref name=":0">{{cite web|url=https://indiankanoon.org/doc/1054471/ |title=The Bihar Reorganisation Act, 2000|website=indiankanoon.org|date=25 August 2000|access-date=31 August 2023}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/thehindu/2000/08/12/stories/01120008.htm|website=[[The Hindu]]|title=Parliament passes Jharkhand bill}} {{dead link|date=April 2021|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref> The law was introduced by the NDA government headed by Prime Minister [[Atal Bihari Vajpayee]] to fulfil an election promise.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Frontline Newsletter {{!}} Poll Vault {{!}} Johar Jharkhand |url=https://frontline.thehindu.com/newsletter/poll-vault-anand-mishra/johar-jharkhand-frontline-newsletter-poll-vault/article68158538.ece |access-date=2025-08-18 |website=frontline.thehindu.com |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Jharkhand: The tribal states political chess game |url=https://www.tribuneindia.com/news/india/jharkhand-the-tribal-states-political-chess-game/ |access-date=2025-08-18 |website=The Tribune |language=en}}</ref>

title: Gaga: Five Foot Two
diff_added:
''Gaga: Five Foot Two'' is a documentary that follows a year in the life of American singer [[Lady Gaga]] at age 30 as she records, releases, and promotes her fifth studio album, ''[[Joanne (album)|Joanne]]''. Framed by footage connected to her performance at the [[Super Bowl LI halftime show]], the film presents an intimate portrait of Gaga's personal and professional life, documenting her interactions with family members, friends, collaborators, employees, and fans. It also foregrounds her struggle with chronic pain, later identified as [[fibromyalgia]],<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/newsbeat-41250806|title=Lady Gaga reveals chronic illness, with fibromyalgia featuring in her new documentary|date=September 13, 2017|access-date=March 25, 2026|publisher=[[BBC]]|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260325143237/https://www.bbc.com/news/newsbeat-41250806|archive-date=March 25, 2026|url-status=live}}</ref> showing how it disrupts her daily routines, rehearsals, performances, and studio work, and tracing her attempts to manage it through treatment and physical therapy. The film also juxtaposes major career moments with more domestic or informal scenes, including Gaga cooking at home, sitting topless by a pool, and speaking candidly about men and the pressures surrounding her public life.
A central focus of the documentary is the creation and promotion of ''Joanne''. Gaga is shown in recording sessions with collaborators including [[Mark Ronson]] and [[BloodPop]], as well as during the making of songs such as "Hey Girl", a duet with [[Florence Welch]]. The film follows several aspects of the album's rollout, including the shooting of the music video for "[[Perfect Illusion]]" in the desert, during which Gaga wonders whether fans will be disappointed by her departure from the elaborate costumes of her earlier career, getting a ''Joanne'' tattoo, the online leak of the album, and a visit to [[Walmart]] to check its placement in stores. A sequence of Gaga interacting with fans is intercut with archival footage of her earlier, more extravagant appearances, highlighting the contrast between her past image and the more stripped-back persona she adopted for ''Joanne''.

title: Tariq Ramadan
comment: Refine lead, clarify background and roles, add convictions and status, tighten wording
diff_added:
'''Tariq Ramadan''' ({{langx|ar|طارق رمضان}}, {{IPA|ar|tˤaːriq ramadˤaːn|}}; born 26 August 1962) is a Swiss Muslim academic, philosopher and writer.<ref name="guardian" /> He has held academic positions including professor of contemporary Islamic studies at [[St Antony's College, Oxford]] and the [[Faculty of Theology and Religion, University of Oxford]].<ref name="ProfessorialFellow"/><ref name="FTR"/> He has also been affiliated with institutions including [[Doshisha University]], [[Université Mundiapolis]], and the Research Centre of Islamic Legislation and Ethics in Doha.<ref name="CileCenter"/>
Ramadan is the grandson of [[Hassan al-Banna]], the founder of the [[Muslim Brotherhood]], and is known  for his writings and lectures on Islam, Muslim identity in Europe, and reformist thought.<ref name="oxford" /> He has described himself as a "[[Salafi movement|Salafi]] reformist".<ref>{{cite magazine | url=https://www.newyorker.com/news/news-desk/how-the-tariq-ramadan-scandal-derailed-the-balancetonporc-movement-in-france | title=How the Tariq Ramadan Scandal Derailed the #Balancetonporc Movement in France | access-date=10 December 2017 | last=Shatz | first=Adam | magazine=The New Yorker | archive-date=9 December 2017 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171209081357/https://www.newyorker.com/news/news-desk/how-the-tariq-ramadan-scandal-derailed-the-balancetonporc-movement-in-france | url-status=live}}</ref>

Ramadan has been convicted of [[rape]] and [[Sexual assault|sexual coercion]] in Switzerland and of rape and rape of a [[Vulnerable adult|vulnerable person]] in France.<ref name="c282"/><ref name="a356"/> In 2024, a Swiss appeals court convicted him in relation to a 2008 incident in Geneva, the conviction which became final in 2025 after his appeal was rejected by the [[Federal Supreme Court of Switzerland]].<ref>{{Cite news |date=10 September 2024 |title=Tariq Ramadan convicted of rape in Switzerland |work=BBC|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cx2n74n6q1vo |access-date=10 September 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |date=2025-08-28 |title=Switzerland's top court upholds Islamic scholar Tariq Ramadan's conviction for rape, sexual coercion |url=https://apnews.com/article/tariq-ramadan-switzerland-rape-dc3897baa9e54a4d629e9f89e6d55677 |access-date=2025-08-28 |website=AP News}}</ref> In March 2026, the [[Tribunal de Paris|Paris criminal court]] convicted him in relation to offences committed between 2009 and 2016 and sentenced him to 18 years' imprisonment; the judgment is not final and may be appealed.<ref name="c282"/><ref name="a356"/>

title: Fragging
diff_added: '''Fragging''' is the deliberate killing or attempted killing of a soldier, usually a superior, by a fellow soldier. [[U.S. military]] personnel coined the word during the [[Vietnam War]], when such killings were most often committed or attempted with a [[fragmentation grenade]],<ref>{{Cite web|title=Frag|url=https://www.oxfordlearnersdictionaries.com/definition/english/frag|access-date=June 21, 2021|website=www.oxfordlearnersdictionaries.com}}</ref> to make it appear that the killing was accidental or during combat with the enemy. The term fragging now encompasses any deliberate killing of military colleagues.<ref>{{cite book |author1=William Darryl Henderson |editor1-last=Chambers |editor1-first=John Whiteclay |title=The Oxford companion to American military history |date=1999 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-507198-6 |page=279 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Rzy_yNMKbcC&pg=PA279 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |author1=William Darryl Henderson |title=Fragging {{!}} Encyclopedia.com |url=https://www.encyclopedia.com/history/encyclopedias-almanacs-transcripts-and-maps/fragging |website=www.encyclopedia.com}}</ref>

title: R. D. Laing
diff_added:
==Laing and anti-psychiatry==

==Works==
In 1913, psychiatrist and philosopher [[Karl Jaspers]] had pronounced in his work [[Karl Jaspers#Contributions to psychiatry|''General Psychopathology'']] that many of the symptoms of mental illness (and particularly of [[delusion]]s) were "un-understandable", and were therefore worthy of little consideration except as a sign of some other underlying primary disorder. Then, in 1956, [[Gregory Bateson]] and his colleagues [[Donald deAvila Jackson|Donald Jackson]] and [[Jay Haley]] articulated a theory of schizophrenia as stemming from [[double bind]] situations where a person receives different or contradictory messages.<ref>Bateson, G., Jackson, D. D., Haley, J. & Weakland, J., 1956, Toward a theory of schizophrenia. (in: ''[[Systems Research and Behavioral Science|Behavioral Science]]'', Vol.1, pp. 251–264)</ref>

The perceived symptoms of schizophrenia were therefore an expression of this distress, and should be valued as a [[catharsis|cathartic]] and transformative experience. Laing argued a similar account for psychoses: that the strange behavior and seemingly confused speech of people undergoing a [[psychosis|psychotic]] episode were ultimately understandable as an attempt to communicate worries and concerns, often in situations where this was not possible or not permitted. Laing stressed the role of society, and particularly the [[family]], in the development of "madness" (his term). Laing saw [[psychopathology]] as being seated not in biological or psychic organs {{emdash}} whereby environment is relegated to playing at most only an accidental role as immediate trigger of disease (the "stress diathesis model" of the nature and causes of psychopathology) {{emdash}} but rather in the social cradle, the urban home, that cultivates it, the very crucible in which selves are forged. This re-evaluation of the locus of the disease process {{emdash}} and consequent shift in forms of treatment {{emdash}} was in stark contrast to psychiatric orthodoxy (in the broadest sense we have of ourselves as psychological subjects and pathological selves). Laing was revolutionary in valuing the content of psychotic behaviour and speech as a valid expression of distress, albeit wrapped in an enigmatic language of personal symbolism which is meaningful only from within their situation. Laing expanded the view of the "[[double bind]]" hypothesis put forth by Bateson and his team, and came up with a new concept to describe the highly complex situation that unfolds in the process of "going mad" {{emdash}} an "incompatible knot". Laing never denied the existence of mental illness, but viewed it in a radically different light from his contemporaries. For Laing, mental illness could be a transformative episode whereby the process of undergoing mental distress was compared to a [[shaman]]ic journey. The traveller could return from the journey with important insights, and may have become (in the views of Laing and his followers) a

===Laing and anti-psychiatry===
His lawyer son Adrian, speaking to ''The Independent'' in September 2008, said, "It was ironic that my father became well known as a family psychiatrist, when, in the meantime, he had nothing to do with his own family". His second daughter Susan died in 1976, aged 21, of [[leukemia|leukaemia]].<ref>{{cite book |last1=Laing |first1=Adrian C. |title=R. D. Laing: a life |date=1997 |publisher=HarperCollins |location=London |isbn=0-00-638829-9 |pages=181–183}}</ref> His third daughter Karen was born in Glasgow in 1955 and is now a retired psychotherapist. Adam, his oldest son by his second marriage, who had been in an increasingly melancholic and fragile state of mind, was found dead in May 2008 in a tent on the island of [[Formentera]]. He had died of a heart attack, aged 41.<ref name="Ob08"/> His fourth daughter Natascha died in 2018 of cancer, aged 48.

== Gameplay ==

''Alpha Polaris'' is a 2.5D point-and-click adventure. [[Geometric modeling|Geometrically modeled]] 3D characters act in front of [[Rendering (computer graphics)|pre-rendered]] backgrounds. The game world is divided into individual game screens through which the player can navigate using the mouse. Also with the mouse the player can interact with the gameworld, examine its elements, pick up objects and apply them to elements of the game world or other objects and interact with [[Non-player character|NPCs]]. Conversations with NPCs take place as [[multiple choice]] dialogues.

== Development ==

''Alpha Polaris'' was developed using the [[Wintermute Engine|Wintermute]] [[game engine]].<ref name="gf">{{cite web |url=http://www.gamefront.com/daily-independent-alpha-polaris-gets-spooky-in-greenland/ |title=Daily Independent: Alpha Polaris Gets Spooky in Greenland |author=Richardson, Ben |date=July 18, 2011 |work=gamefront.com |publisher=Break Media |accessdate=September 9, 2011 |archive-date=July 22, 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110722233442/http://www.gamefront.com/daily-independent-alpha-polaris-gets-spooky-in-greenland/ |url-status=dead }}</ref>

diff_added: In broader accounts of [[Surrealism in Japan|Japanese Surrealism]] and its visual arts, the 1930s are often discussed as forming an important link between prewar and postwar avant-gardes, even when overt “avant-garde” identification had been made difficult under wartime conditions.<ref name="Stojkovic2020p5">{{cite book |last=Stojkovic |first=Jelena |title=Surrealism and Photography in 1930s Japan: The Impossible Avant-Garde |publisher=Routledge |year=2020 |isbn=978-1788314053 |page=5}}</ref> Stojkovic argues that Surrealist ideas remained central to postwar avant-garde art in Japan beyond the 1930s, extending beyond but also including photography.<ref name="Stojkovic2020p177">{{cite book |last=Stojkovic |first=Jelena |title=Surrealism and Photography in 1930s Japan: The Impossible Avant-Garde |publisher=Routledge |year=2020 |isbn=978-1788314053 |page=177}}</ref> Within this longer arc, [[Kansuke Yamamoto (artist)|Yamamoto]] is described as resuming activities after the war and continuing to participate in later photographic circles.<ref name="Stojkovic2020p183">{{cite book |last=Stojkovic |first=Jelena |title=Surrealism and Photography in 1930s Japan: The Impossible Avant-Garde |publisher=Routledge |year=2020 |isbn=978-1788314053 |page=183}}</ref> In Nagoya, an early postwar instance of such renewed activity was [[VIVI (photography group)|VIVI]], founded in 1947 by [[Keiichirō Gotō]] and his collaborators; a [[Metropolitan Museum of Art]] collection note describes the group as reviving the city's avant-garde scene after wartime governmental censure of experimental practice.<ref>{{cite web |title=Memorandum |url=https://www.metmuseum.org/ja/art/collection/search/919810 |website=The Metropolitan Museum of Art |access-date=2026-03-26}}</ref> One postwar framework for such continued experimentation was the [[Japan Subjective Photography League]], founded in May 1956, through which prewar avant-garde photographers including [[Kansuke Yamamoto (artist)|Kansuke Yamamoto]], [[Keiichirō Gotō]], and [[Kōrō Honjō]] were briefly regrouped alongside younger postwar figures such as [[Kiyoji Ōtsuji]], [[Ikkō Narahara]], and [[Yasuhiro Ishimoto]] in the First International Subjective Photography Exhibition later that year.<ref>{{cite web |title=主観主義写真（主観的写真） |trans-title=Subjectivist Photography (Subjective Photography) |url=https://artscape.jp/artword/6073/ |access-date=2026-03-22 |website=artscape |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="Fujimura2022p186">{{cite book |last=Fujimura |first=Satomi |title=[[Avant-Garde Rising: The Photographic Vanguard in Modern Japan]] |publisher=Kokusho Kankokai |year=2022 |isbn=9784336073679 |editor=[[Tokyo Photographic Art Museum]] |location=Tokyo |page=195 |language=ja |chapter=前衛写真の終焉 |trans-chapter=The End of Avant-Garde Photography}}</ref><ref name="KanekoJMD172">{{cite book |last=Kaneko |first=Ryūichi |author-link=Ryūichi Kaneko |title=[[Japan's Modern Divide: The Photographs of Hiroshi Hamaya and Kansuke Yamamoto]] |publisher=[[

title: Paul Delmas-Marsalet

'''Paul Delmas-Marsalet''', who was born on 4 August 1898 in [[Dax, Landes|Dax]] and died on 29 July 1977 in [[Léognan]]<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last=Bourgeois |first=Marc L. |date=2017-09-01 |title=Paul Delmas-Marsalet (1898–1977) |url=https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0003448717302202 |journal=Annales Médico-psychologiques, revue psychiatrique |volume=175 |issue=7 |page=674–675 |doi=10.1016/j.amp.2017.07.004 |issn=0003-4487 |access-date=2026-02-26}}</ref>, was a French physician and professor of [[neuropsychiatry]] at the [[University of Bordeaux]], who played a major role in the development of clinical and experimental [[neuroscience]]<ref name=":1">{{Cite journal |last=Le Moal |first=Michel |last2=Battin |first2=Jacques |last3=Bioulac |first3=Bernard |last4=Bourgeois |first4=Marc Louis |date=2008 |title=[Neurosciences in Bordeaux] |url=https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19024950 |journal=Bulletin De l'Academie Nationale De Medecine |volume=192 |issue=4 |page=817–831 |issn=0001-4079 |pmid=19024950 |access-date=2026-02-26}}</ref>.

It lasted approximately six minutes and caused a [[tsunami]]. title: Competition Act, 2002
diff_added:
| short_title     = Competition Act, 2002

The '''Competition Act, 2002''' was enacted by the [[Parliament of India]] and governs Indian competition law. It replaced [[The Monopolies and Restrictive Trade Practices Act, 1969]]. Under this legislation, the [[Competition Commission of India]] was established to prevent the activities that adversely affected competition in India.<ref>{{Cite web|url = http://indiankanoon.org/doc/1113485/|title = Sub-section 1 of Section 7 of Competition Act 2002|access-date = 3 November 2015|publisher = Indian Kanoon}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|title = CCI will be in full operation next year|url = http://www.thehindu.com/todays-paper/tp-business/cci-will-be-in-full-operation-next-year/article1909354.ece|newspaper = The Hindu|date = 2007-09-11|access-date = 2015-11-19|issn = 0971-751X|language = en-IN}}</ref> This act extends to the entirety of India.

title: Leprosy in Japan
diff_added:
Mieko Kamiya (1914–1979) was a psychiatrist who treated leprosy patients at Nagashima Aiseien Sanatorium. She was known for the translation of books on philosophy. She was said to be "one of doctors of the Department of psychiatry of Tokyo University soon after the war", who "greatly helped the Ministry of Education and the General Headquarters as English-speaking secretary", and to be "an adviser to [[Empress Michiko]]". She wrote many books as a highly educated, multi-lingual person. One of her books titled for the meaning of life ([[Ikigai]] in Japanese) was based on her experiences with leprosy patients and attracted many readers.
Takekichi Sugai (菅井竹吉, 1871–1944) was the first chief doctor at Sotojima Hoyoen Sanatorium (1909–1923) who wrote many papers on leprosy. Sotojima Hoyoen was a public leprosy sanatorium in [[Osaka Prefecture]] and functional between 1909 and 1934.
Koh Yasuda (保田耕, 1907–1943) was a Japanese ophthalmologist who became the first director of Amami Wakoen Sanatorium in [[Amami Ōshima]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. He was drafted into military service and died in China in 1943.

title: Teleilat el-Ghassul
diff_added: The eight-rayed 'Star of Ghassul' or 'Ghassulian star' is some 6,000 years old, counting among the earliest examples of the [[fresco]] technique, and was discovered in 1932,<ref name= IMJstar>{{cite news |title= Special Display: The Star of Ghassul |publisher=[[Israel Museum]] |url= https://www.imj.org.il/en/exhibitions/special-display-star-ghassul |access-date= 26 March 2026}}</ref> during the 1929-38 PBI excavations at Ghassul, as the centerpiece<ref name= ToIstar>{{cite news |last= Ben Zion |first= Ilan |title= Mysterious 6,000-year-old star mural sees first daylight in Jerusalem |date= 13 November 2016 |work=[[The Times of Israel]] |url= https://www.timesofisrael.com/mysterious-6000-year-old-star-mural-sees-first-daylight-in-jerusalem/ |access-date= 26 March 2026}}</ref> of a very large wall painting in one of the houses discovered at the site.<ref name= IMJstar/> The star has a diameter of 1.84 meters and was painted on a light-colored plaster with red, black, white, and yellow pigments.<ref name= IMJstar/> The fact that it was surrounded by masked figures and animals suggested to some researchers that it represented the sun, which was venerated as a major deity, this view challenging the notion that humans of that period hadn't yet developed the concept of abstract gods.<ref name= ToIstar/> More cautious researchers presume it to represents either the sun or another celestial body, possibly associated with the seasons or the cycle of life, and see it as perhaps it symbolizing a deity.<ref name= IMJstar/> The room containing the mural is interpreted as a ritual shrine within a regular dwelling.<ref name= IMJstar/> In 1936 the mural's remains were removed from the site and and ended up in a storeroom of what is today the [[Rockefeller name= ToIstar/> Excavations in the 1970s produced an additional section of the murals, now held by [[The Jordan Museum]] in [[Amman]].<ref name= ToIstar/> The star was briefly put on display during a temporary exhibition at the new [[Israel Antiquities Authority]] [[National Campus for the Archaeology of Israel| headquarters]] in 2016<ref name= ToIstar/>, and again after restoration and preservation work, at the Rockefeller Museum in 2021-2023.<ref name= IMJstar/>

title: Cardiovascular disease in women
comment: Alter: journal, pages. Add: hdl, bibcode, pmc, pmid, volume. Formatted [[WP:ENDASH|dashes]]. | [[:en:WP:UCB|Use this tool]]. [[:en:WP:DBUG|Report bugs]]. | #UCB_Gadget
diff_added:
Since the mid-1980s, CVD has been the leading cause of death in women, despite being presumed to be a primarily male disease. Two types of CVDs are shown to be the leading causes of death in women globally, according to the [[World Health Organization]]: [[ischemic heart disease]] and [[stroke]].<ref name="World Health Organization-2024">{{cite press release |title=The top 10 causes of death |url=https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/the-top-10-causes-of-death |publisher=World Health Organization |date=7 August 2024 }}</ref> Although, on average, women will develop CVD 5-10 years later than men, the overall number of CVD diagnoses in men and women is similar.<ref name="Conrad">{{cite journal |last1=Conrad |first1=Nathalie |last2=Molenberghs |first2=Geert |last3=Verbeke |first3=Geert |last4=Zaccardi |first4=Francesco |last5=Lawson |first5=Claire |last6=Friday |first6=Jocelyn M |last7=Su |first7=Huimin |last8=Jhund |first8=Pardeep S |last9=Sattar |first9=Naveed |last10=Rahimi |first10=Kazem |last11=Cleland |first11=John G |last12=Khunti |first12=Kamlesh |last13=Budts |first13=Werner |last14=McMurray |first14=John J V |title=Trends in cardiovascular disease incidence among 22 million people in the UK over 20 years: population based study |journal=BMJ |date=26 June 2024 |volume=385 |article-number=e078523 |doi=10.1136/bmj-2023-078523 |pmid=38925788 |pmc=11203392 }}</ref>
CVD is the leading cause of death in women worldwide.<ref name="World Health Organization-2024" /> Studies have shown a recent successful decline in CVD mortality overall. However, there is a strong indication for male-specific reductions being implemented in practice, thus, female mortality remains high, despite the incidence of CVD being lower.<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Bots |first1=Sophie H |last2=Peters |first2=Sanne A E |last3=Woodward |first3=Mark |title=Sex differences in coronary heart disease and stroke mortality: a global assessment of the effect of ageing between 1980 and 2010 |journal=BMJ Global Health |date=March 2017 |volume=2 |issue=2 |article-number=e000298 |doi=10.1136/bmjgh-2017-000298 |pmid=28589033 |pmc=5435266 }}</ref><ref name="Gao-2019">{{cite journal |last1=Gao |first1=Zujie |last2=Chen |first2=Zengsheng |last3=Sun |first3=Anqiang |last4=Deng |first4=Xiaoyan |title=Gender differences in cardiovascular disease |journal=Medicine in Novel Technology and Devices |date=December 2019 |volume=4 |article-number=100025 |doi=10.1016/j.medntd.2019.100025 |doi-access=free }}</ref> The number of women surviving and dying from CVD far outweighs the number of men in comparison.<ref>{{cite journal | vauthors = Mosca L, Barrett-Connor E, Wenger NK | title = Sex/gender differences in cardiovascular disease prevention: what a difference a decade makes | journal = Circulation | volume = 124 | issue = 19 | pages = 2145–2154 | date = November 2011 | pmid = 22064958 | pmc = 3362050 | doi = 10.1161/CIRCULATIONAHA.110.968792 | bibcode = 2011Circu.124.2145M }}</ref>

title: Ikariam
comment: Removed Maintenance Template from "Alliances" and re-wrote the entire section with additional and more specific details from Help section of the game (cannot be linked).  I am editing this content as an employee of Gameforge
diff_added:
''Ikariam'' is nominally [[free-to-play]] but players may opt to purchase in-game advantages via a [[Microtransaction#In MMORPGs|Microtransaction]]<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.worldsinmotion.biz/2008/08/online_world_atlas_ikariam_pt_2.php|title=Online World Atlas: Ikariam -- Pt. 3, Conclusion|author=K., Matthew|publisher=[[Gamasutra|Worlds in Motion]]|date=2008-08-19|access-date=2009-02-11|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090221103335/http://www.worldsinmotion.biz/2008/08/online_world_atlas_ikariam_pt_2.php|archive-date=2009-02-21|url-status=dead}}</ref> system based on Ambrosia, the game's premium currency, which allows them to speed up processes and gain various advantages, including access to Ikariam Plus.
Once the Academy Research “Foreign Cultures”, which can be found in the “Seafaring” area of the Academy, is completed, an Embassy can be built. From the Embassy building, players can found their own alliance or apply to join an existing one. Alliances are groups of players who cooperate and support each other.<ref name="Ikariam vs. Travian" /> They will usually protect one another; if a member is attacked, stronger members or the entire alliance may intervene to defend against the attacker.

The maximum number of members an alliance can have depends on the level of the leader’s Embassy. Additionally, alliances generate a small daily bonus of resources based on their total number of members.

Each alliance includes a leadership structure consisting of one leader and several special roles. The leader has full administrative control and can distribute special posts, transfer leadership, edit alliance details (such as name, tag, language, and description), and disband the alliance. The leader can also manage diplomatic points by transferring free personal diplomatic points to the alliance or returning unused alliance diplomatic points to their account. Like all members, the leader benefits from the alliance’s resource bonus.

There are three assignable special roles within an alliance: Diplomat, General, and Home Secretary.

The Diplomat is responsible for managing external relations. This role can edit the public alliance page and receives messages sent to the alliance from external players or other alliances.

The General oversees military coordination. This includes viewing the total troop strength of each member, monitoring incoming attacks against alliance members, and managing alliance military actions, including the ability to withdraw attacks initiated by members.

The Home Secretary manages internal organization. This role can view the total resources of each member, create and manage custom alliance ranks, assign those ranks to members, and edit the internal alliance page. The Home Secretary also receives and manages applications from players wishing to join the alliance.

title: Iran: A Modern History
comment: Split reviews into individual paragraphs for readability, added wikilink for Justin Marozzi
diff_added:
''Iran: A Modern History'' was generally well received by critics. Fatima Raza from ''Strategic Studies'' is unequivocally positive, endorsing the book as a "masterpiece" and an "invigorating read" for those with prior knowledge of the region. She believes it successfully combines detailed articulation with rigorous analysis, creating a work that stands at par with ''[[Shahnameh]]'' on [[Persian civilization]].<ref>{{Cite magazine|title=Review|last=Raza|first=Fatima|date=Spring 2019|magazine=Strategic Studies|publisher=[[Institute of Strategic Studies Islamabad]]|issue=1|volume=39|pages=110–112|jstor=48544294}}</ref>

Steve Donoghue writing for ''[[Open Letters Monthly]]'' offers also a highly positive assessment of the book, characterizing it as a "magisterial" and essential text. Donoghue endorses the book as "essential reading," particularly for Western audiences unfamiliar with this history, citing its broad scope, deep detail, and surprisingly readable narrative style. The final verdict is that the work's value lies not only in its comprehensive historical account but also in Amanat's masterful storytelling, which elevates the text to offer readers a profound and engaging historical experience.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.openlettersmonthlyarchive.com/olm/book-review-iran-a-modern-history|title=Book Review: Iran: A Modern History|accessdate=30 October 2025|website=Open Letters Monthly Archive|last=Donoghue|first=Steve}}</ref>

Monica Ringer of ''[[International Journal of Middle East Studies]]'' notes two primary shortcomings: first, the absence of footnotes constitutes a significant drawback for scholars, a lacuna only partially compensated for by the annotated bibliography; and second, she describes the prevalence of [[typography|typographical]] errors as "distracting" for such a seminal work. Despite these editorial and production flaws, she is overwhelmingly positive, declaring ''Iran'' to be "a milestone" that "will continue to be required reading in Iranian history for generations to come." Ringer affirms its value for both undergraduate students and specialist scholars, praising Amanat's interpretive insights and his successful synthesis of a vast and complex field.<ref>{{Cite magazine|title=Review|last=Ringer|first=Monica|date=February 2019|magazine=[[International Journal of Middle East Studies]]|publisher=[[Cambridge University Press]]|issue=1|volume=51|pages=161–163|jstor=26852762}}</ref>

Paul Lay delivers a highly favorable assessment of ''Iran'', positioning the book as a monumental and invaluable scholarly work that provides essential context for understanding contemporary Iran. Lay argues that the book's primary achievement is revealing the deep, complex, and often contradictory cultural and historical foundations that have shaped the nation over five centuries, moving beyond the typical Western focus on 20th-century political events. title: Mike Nifong
comment: /* Duke lacrosse case */ Rework Okrent segue
diff_added:
In 2006, Nifong pursued [[rape]], [[sexual assault]], and [[kidnapping]] charges against Reade Seligmann, Collin Finnerty, and David Evans, three white members of the [[Duke University]] [[Duke Blue Devils men's lacrosse|men's lacrosse team]]. The accusation of sexual assault was made by [[Crystal Mangum]], one of two local black women hired by the lacrosse team to work as strippers for a party.<ref name="abcnews">{{cite web|publisher=[[ABC News (United States)|ABC News]]|title=Duke Lacrosse Scandal Sheds New Light on the Stripper Industry: A Campus Trend?|url=https://abcnews.go.com/US/LegalCenter/story?id=1882072&page=1|access-date=November 4, 2006}}</ref> The case attracted national and international media attention.<ref>{{cite news|last1=Neff|first1=J.|title=Nifong conduct rebuked early|newspaper=[[News & Observer]]|date=January 15, 2007|url=http://www.newsobserver.com/2007/01/15/44563/nifong-conduct-rebuked-early.html|location=Raleigh, NC|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131218131604/http://www.newsobserver.com/2007/01/15/44563/nifong-conduct-rebuked-early.html|archive-date=December 18, 2013}}</ref>

[[Daniel Okrent]], recently public editor of the ''[[New York Times]]'', wrote that the Duke case "conformed too well to too many preconceived notions of too many in the press: white over black, rich over poor, athletes over non-athletes, men over women, educated over non-educated."<ref>{{cite journal|last1=Smolkin|first1=R.|title=Justice Delayed|journal=[[American Journalism Review]]|year=2007|volume=29|issue=August/September|pages=18–21|url=http://ajrarchive.org/article.asp?id=4379}}</ref>

title: Franz Josef Hamm
diff_added:
'''Franz Josef Hamm''' (born 2 May 1936) is a German architect. After designing buildings influenced by [[Le Corbusier]], his work shifted to focus on historic preservation and building conservation—particularly of half-timbered structures. His pioneering work, together with Hildegard Schirmacher and Walter Neuhäusser, for the old town of [[Limburg an der Lahn|Limburg]] became a model for other towns, including the [[New Frankfurt Old Town]].
Hamm is regarded as a pioneer in old town revitalization, particularly in the field of half-timbered house reconstruction. In collaboration with {{ill|Hildegard Schirmacher|de}} and Neuhäusser he renovated Limburg's old town professionally, serving as a model for other projects such as the revitalization of [[Marburg]]'s old town.<ref name="Kramm" /> Based on an architectural partnership with Jörg Torsten Brod, Hamm ran an architectural firm from 2005 to 2014 that was responsible, among other things, for the renovation of half-timbered houses in the [[New Frankfurt Old Town]].<ref name="Kramm" />
* 2009–2011: Frankfurt, buildings in [[New Frankfurt Old Town]]

title: Indiana Superior Courts
diff_added: The Superior Court is an additional court created by the [[Indiana General Assembly]] in response to the growing local need for more trial courts. Most Indiana trial courts are Superior Courts, and nearly all Indiana counties have a Superior Court in addition to their Circuit Court. The Superior Court will handle small claims and minor offenses in the absence of a county court system.<ref>{{Cite web |date=January 1, 2003 |title=The Indiana Trial Court System |url=https://www.extension.purdue.edu/extmedia/4H/4-H-971.pdf |website=Purdue University|access-date=2026-03-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150908084343/https://www.extension.purdue.edu/extmedia/4H/4-H-971.pdf|archive-date=2015-09-08}}</ref>

title: CALM3
diff_added:
CALM-3 is best known for contracting the heart muscles, and depending on whether this activity is consistent or not, other diseases could emerge as a downside. It is able to maintain or regulate in different types of biological systems, such as [[cytokinesis]] or the centrosome cycle.<ref>{{Cite web |title=CALM3 - Calmodulin-3 - Homo sapiens (Human) - CALM3 gene & protein |url=https://www.uniprot.org/uniprot/P0DP25 |access-date=2022-05-18 |website=www.uniprot.org |language=en}}</ref>
Calmodulin-3 is able to perform different types of activities and roles, such as binding of calcium and significant activity in regulating an enzyme.<ref name="uniprot.org">{{Cite web |title=CALM3 - Calmodulin-3 - Homo sapiens (Human) - CALM3 gene & protein |url=https://www.uniprot.org/uniprot/P0DP25 |access-date=2022-04-16 |website=www.uniprot.org |language=en}}</ref> The gene CALM-3 is likely to contribute to illnesses that may lead to death, such as Ventricular tachycardia which is associated with the [[ventricular tachycardia]] functioning in 2 directions and long QT syndrome which is associated with the [[QT interval]] in the electrocardiogram that is significantly longer than normal.<ref name="uniprot.org"/> In its structure, there are 2 helices that are observed in each of its helix-loop-helix and are then shaped into a perpendicular pattern due to the surface of the protein changing over time.<ref>{{cite journal | vauthors = Zhang M, Yuan T | title = Molecular mechanisms of calmodulin's functional versatility | journal = Biochemistry and Cell Biology | volume = 76 | issue = 2–3 | pages = 313–323 | date = 2011-01-24 | pmid = 9923700 | doi = 10.1139/o98-027 }}</ref> Through transcription, the gene CALM-3 is able to perform the activity of a regulator for its own gene expression and has 6 exons, indicating that each exon has a specific function that takes place in the initiation stage.<ref>{{cite journal | vauthors = Koller M, Schnyder B, Strehler EE | title = Structural organization of the human CaMIII calmodulin gene | journal = Biochimica et Biophysica Acta (BBA) - Gene Structure and Expression | volume = 1087 | issue = 2 | pages = 180–189 | date = October 1990 | pmid = 2223880 | doi = 10.1016/0167-4781(90)90203-E }}</ref> If there are potentially variants that could impact the calmodulin protein, it could affect the concentration of the Ca mediators that are a part of the protein.<ref>{{cite journal | vauthors = Friedrich FW, Bausero P, Sun Y, Treszl A, Krämer E, Juhr D, Richard P, Wegscheider K, Schwartz K, Brito D, Arbustini E, Waldenström A, Isnard R, Komajda M, Eschenhagen T, Carrier L | display-authors = 6 | title = A new polymorphism in human calmodulin III gene promoter is a potential modifier gene for familial hypertrophic cardiomyopathy | journal = European Heart Journal | volume = 30 | issue = 13 | pages = 1648–1655 | date = July 2009 | pmid = 19429631 | doi = 10.1093/eurheartj/ehp153 | doi-access = free }}</ref>

title: Wang Dao
diff_added:
'''Wang Dao''' ({{zh|t=王導|w=Wang Tao}}; 276{{efn|According to Wang Dao's biography in ''Book of Jin'', he was 64 (by East Asian reckoning) when he died.<ref>(咸康五年薨，时年六十四。) ''Jin Shu'', vol.65</ref>. Thus by calculation, his birth year should be 276.}} – 7 September 339<ref>According to Emperor Cheng's biography in ''Book of Jin'', Wang Dao died on the ''gengshen'' day of the 7th month of the 5th year of the ''Xiankang'' era of his reign. This corresponds to 7 Sep 339 in the Julian calendar.{{efn|[(咸康五年)秋七月庚申，使持节、侍中、丞相、领扬州刺史、始兴公王导薨] ''Jin Shu'', vol. 07</ref>}}), [[courtesy name]] '''Maohong''' (茂弘), formally '''Duke Wenxian of Shixing''' (始興文獻公), was a Chinese politician during the [[Jin Dynasty (266–420)|Jin dynasty]] who played an important role in the administrations of [[Emperor Yuan of Jin|Emperor Yuan]], [[Emperor Ming of Jin|Emperor Ming]], and [[Emperor Cheng of Jin|Emperor Cheng]], including as Emperor Cheng's [[regent]]. In these capacities, he served as a crucial governing figure of the [[Eastern Jin Dynasty]] during its first decades, as well as the leading member of the prominent [[Wang clan of Langya]], especially after the death of his rebellious cousin [[Wang Dun]]. His governance style was to be lenient with the laws, and he handed out few punishments—which stabilized the Jin regime greatly, but which also led to extensive, if moderate, corruption and incompetence in the Jin regime, making it difficult for Jin armies to recapture northern China.
==Anecdotes=
When Huan Yi (father of [[Huan Wen]]) first reached Sima Rui's domain from the north, he saw the weak Eastern Jin court and expressed his despair to [[Zhou Yi (Jin dynasty)|Zhou Yi]]. However, he then met Wang Dao and the pair talked about current affairs. When Huan next met Zhou, he told the latter, "I have met [[Guan Zhong|Guan Yiwu]]; I am no longer worried."<ref>(晋国既建，以导为丞相军谘祭酒。桓彝初过江，见朝廷微弱，谓周𫖮曰：“我以中州多故，来此欲求全活，而寡弱如此，将何以济！”忧惧不乐。往见导，极谈世事，还，谓𫖮曰：“向见管夷吾，无复忧矣。”) ''Jin Shu'', vol.65</ref>

In these capacities, he served as a crucial governing figure of the [[Eastern Jin Dynasty]] during its first decades, as well as the leading member of the prominent [[Wang clan of Langya]], especially after the death of his rebellious cousin [[Wang Dun]]. title: Jindra Tichá
diff_added:
'''Jindra Tichá''' (born 31 March 1937) is a Czech-born academic and writer living in [[New Zealand]].<ref name=portal/> In 2012, she was voted the 11th most influential Czech expatriate by the Czech public.<ref name=otago>{{cite news |url=http://www.odt.co.nz/campus/university-otago/229077/czech-dunedin-writer-honoured-homeland |title=Czech Dunedin writer honoured in homeland |newspaper=[[Otago Daily Times]] |date=6 October 2012}}</ref>
She was born in [[Prague]] and studied logic at [[Charles University]] in Prague, earning her [[Doctor of Philosophy|PhD]] there. She married [[Pavel Tichý]].<ref name=portal/> After she and her husband were declared enemies of the state by the invading Russians<ref name=otago/> in 1969, the couple moved to [[Exeter]] in England and then, in 1970, went to New Zealand. She lectured on philosophy and political science at the [[University of Otago]] where her husband also lectured.<ref name=portal/>
Tichá first published short stories in the 1980s through the Canadian-based Czech publishing house [[68 Publishers]]. It was not until after 1989 that her books began to appear in the [[Czech Republic]].<ref name=portal>{{cite web|url=http://www.czechlit.cz/en/authors/ticha-jindra/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20150223203815/http://www.czechlit.cz/en/authors/ticha-jindra/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=2015-02-23 |title=Jindra Tichá |work=Czech literature portal}}</ref>

'''Dahanu''' (Pronunciation: [[Help:IPA/Marathi|[ɖəɦaːɳuː]]]) is a [[coast]]al town and a [[municipal council]] in [[Palghar district]] of [[Maharashtra|Maharashtra state]] in [[Konkan division]]. title: Phil Regan (baseball)
diff_added:
Regan earned varsity letters in [[basketball]], [[American football|football]], and [[baseball]] at Wayland High School in [[Wayland, Michigan]]. After one year at [[Western Michigan University]], he signed with the [[Detroit Tigers]] in 1956. He compiled a 61–42 record and 3.76 [[earned run average]] as a [[starting pitcher]] in the Tigers' [[farm system]] before earning a call up to the majors midway through the 1960 season.
He made his major league debut on July 19 against the [[Washington Senators (1901–60)|Washington Senators]]. Entering the game already behind 3–0, he allowed just two [[hit (baseball)|hits]] in five [[inning]]s of work, including a [[home run]] to [[Harmon Killebrew]].<ref>{{cite web |date=July 19, 1960 |title=Washington Senators 5, Detroit Tigers 0 |url=https://www.baseball-reference.com/boxes/DET/DET196007190.shtml |access-date=2025-02-18 |website=[[Baseball-Reference.com]]}}</ref>
He made his first career start in the second game of a [[Doubleheader (baseball)|doubleheader]] with the [[Baltimore Orioles]] on July 23. He left the game with two outs in the ninth inning with the score tied at three, and the bases loaded. [[Relief pitcher]] [[Clem Labine]] [[base on balls|walked]] [[Jim Gentile]] to drive in the winning [[run (baseball)|run]], and hand Regan his first career loss.<ref>{{cite web |date=July 23, 1960 |title=Baltimore Orioles 4, Detroit Tigers 3 |url=https://www.baseball-reference.com/boxes/BAL/BAL196007232.shtml |access-date=2025-02-18 |website=Baseball-Reference.com}}</ref> Splitting his time between starts and relief appearances, he was 0–4 with a 4.50 ERA his rookie season. His first career win also came against Baltimore during his second season in the big leagues, when he went 10–7 with a 5.25 ERA.<ref>{{cite web |date=April 30, 1961 |title=Detroit Tigers 8, Baltimore Orioles 2 |url=https://www.baseball-reference.com/boxes/DET/DET196104301.shtml |access-date=2025-02-18 |website=Baseball-Reference.com}}</ref>
Over six seasons, Regan went 42–44 with a 4.50 ERA for the Detroit Tigers. He was 0–4 with a 4.99 ERA in 1965 when he was demoted to triple A [[Syracuse Chiefs|Syracuse]]. He earned a call up that September, and made two appearances, but it was while he was with Syracuse that Regan learned of interest in him from the Dodgers organization.<ref>{{cite news |author=Bock |first=Hal |date=August 2, 1966 |title=Phil Regan Helps Dodgers To Hold Lead; Bucs One Game back, Giants Keep Pace |url=https://news.google.com/newspapers?nid=2209&dat=19660802&id=16wrAAAAIBAJ&pg=5119,3171720&hl=en |access-date=2025-02-18 |via=Google News Archive |newspaper=[[The Telegraph (Nashua)]]}}</ref>
With Sandy Koufax, [[Don Drysdale]], [[Claude Osteen]], and [[Don Sutton]] in the starting rotation, Regan was used strictly as a reliever in 1966. He responded by going 14–1 with a 1.62 ERA, 88 strikeouts in 116 [[innings pitched]] and a [[National League (baseball)|National League]] leading 21 [[save (baseball)|saves]] to help

title: Jeongjo of Joseon
diff_added:
'''Jeongjo''' ({{Korean|hangul=정조|hanja=正祖}}; 28 October 1752 – 18 August 1800), personal name '''Yi San''' ({{Korean|hangul=이산|hanja=李祘|labels=no}}), sometimes called '''Jeongjo the Great''' ({{Korean|hangul=정조대왕|hanja=正祖大王|labels=no}}), was the 22nd monarch of the [[Joseon|Joseon dynasty]] of [[Korea]]. He was the second son of [[Crown Prince Sado]] and [[Lady Hyegyŏng]], and succeeded his grandfather, [[Yeongjo of Joseon|King Yeongjo]], in 1776.
His father, [[Crown Prince Sado]], was executed in 1762, leading to conflicts over Yi San's legitimacy as heir. As king, he sought to clear his father's name, balance political factions, and strengthen royal authority. He built the [[Hwaseong Fortress]], reformed governance, and promoted talented scholars. Facing assassination attempts, he created the Changyongyeong royal guards. Jeongjo established the [[Kyujanggak|Kyujanggak royal library]], expanded social mobility, and encouraged [[Neo-Confucianism|Neo-Confucian]] scholarship. His reign marked a cultural and political renaissance, but factional struggles persisted. He is also remembered for his various efforts to reform and improve the nation. The era of Jeongjo's rule is considered one of the highest points of the [[Joseon|Joseon era]] and today he is generally considered to be among the greatest leaders in Korean history.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-01-07 |title=“조선 최고 성군은 세종, 인간 승리 드라마는 정조” |url=https://weekly.donga.com/people/article/all/11/1599724/1 |access-date=2025-10-12 |website=주간동아 |language=ko}}</ref>

title: ČT3
comment: clean up, [[WP:AWB/T|typo(s) fixed]]: covid-19 → COVID-19, televize → televise
diff_added:
'''ČT3''' (Česká televise 3, colloquially ''Trojka'') was a Czech [[Public broadcasting|public]] television channel, operated by [[Czech Television]]. ČT3 broadcast originally in 1993 when it replaced the previous station [[OK3 (television)|OK3]]. Unlike the other two channels of the Czech Television at the time, ČT1 and ČT2, ČT3 broadcast its program largely in foreign languages and 24 hours a day.
Establishment of the temporary TV channel ČT3 was announced by Czech Television on 18 March 2020. According to Czech Television (ČT), senior citizens, who were a vulnerable group during the COVID-19 pandemic, spent the most time at home during the crisis and public television wanted to provide them with a television program adapted to their needs. Due to the extraordinary situation, ČT stopped producing most of its regular programs and was able to use the funds for the creation of a new channel.<ref>{{cite web |title=Start ČT3 – Televize třetího věku: Česká televize začne vysílání programu pro seniory |url=https://www.ceskatelevize.cz/vse-o-ct/press/tiskove-zpravy/?id=9133|publisher=[[Czech Television]]|access-date=22 March 2023|language=cs}}</ref> Broadcasting of ČT3 started on 23 March 2020 at 9 a.m.

title: Benedict Ong
diff_added:
Ong first came into controversy when he launched a complaint against fellow councillor John Chambers, whom he claimed made discriminatory comments about Dunedin's South Asian community, on 19 December. Despite a request from Chambers to stay silent on the issue to avoid compromising the investigation, Ong spoke again on the issue on 13 January, where he requested Chambers apologise. On 5 February, a code of conduct complaint was filed against Ong by Sandy Graham, which Ong said was "no big surprise" on Facebook. The original complaint towards Chambers was dismissed 11 February. Ong replied on Facebook, stating he believed the independent investigation had not been independent.
A health and safety assessment left Ong's access to the council building restricted temporarily on 16 February, after concerns were raised that Ong had been physically confronting staff members. Ong then recorded and published a video of himself walking through the building with grey tape over his mouth three days later. Ong continued to wear his tape during a council meeting, from which he was later escorted by fellow councillors.
On 22 February, Ong released a letter he received from [[Sophie Barker (mayor)|Sophie Barker]], in which she stated Ong had been "extremely disrespectful". Ong was removed from his position as deputy lead of the technology portfolio. He stated he would continue to wear his tape at council meetings, later appearing at the [[Dunedin Public Art Gallery]] wearing his tape, releasing photos online asking the gallery to stock "Councillor Ong duct tape" as the "ultimate political performative piece".
The city council voted 10-2 on 26 March to request Ong resign, which he rejected. During the meeting, Ong chose to read from [[The Trial]] instead of defending himself.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.rnz.co.nz/news/national/590566/dunedin-councillor-benedict-ong-asked-to-resign-after-code-of-conduct-breach |title=Dunedin councillor Benedict Ong asked to resign after code of conduct breach |publisher=RNZ |date=26 March 2026}}</ref>

title: Randal Alexander McDonnell, 10th Earl of Antrim
diff_added:
==Early life and education==
April 12, 2015, accessed 3 July 2022</ref>
After Oxford, Dunluce, as he then was, embarked on a career in [[asset management]] in the City of London. In 1992, when he was twenty-five, his father put him in charge of the Glenarm Castle estate, and in that role he invested in Glenarm Shorthorn Beef, Glenarm Organic Salmon and hydroelectric power.<ref name=HC/> Glenarm Castle was first built in 1603 by Antrim's ancestor [[Randal MacDonnell, 1st Earl of Antrim|Randal MacSorley MacDonnell]].<ref>Richard Pococke, John McVeagh, ''Richard Pococke's Irish Tours'' (Irish Academic Press, 1995), p. 212</ref><ref>[[Mark Bence-Jones]], ''Burke's Guide to Country Houses: Volume I, Ireland'' (1978), p. 135</ref>
He was an investment manager at NCL Investments from 1993 to 1997. In 1998 he began to work at Sarasin & Partners LLP, where he has been chairman since 2008,<ref name=Today/><ref>''The Waterlow Stock Exchange Yearbook: Incorporating Crawfords Directory'' (London: Waterlow Professional Publishing, 1 April 2009, {{ISBN|978-1857830897}}), p. 1/17</ref> taking responsibility for the investment mandates of the firm's largest customers and its charitable endowments. He managed the creation of the Sarrasin presence in [[Dublin]] and is also a [[non-executive director]] of Aberdeen Standard Asia Focus PLC.<ref name=Spears>[https://www.spears500.com/adviser/5914/randal-dunluce "Randal Dunluce"], ''[[Spear's Wealth Management Survey]]'', accessed 15 September 2023</ref> In 2002, he was a member of three [[Gentlemen's club|London clubs]], the [[Savile Club|Savile]], the [[Turf Club (gentlemen's club)|Turf]], and the [[Beefsteak Club|Beefsteak]].<ref>"DUNLUCE , Viscount Randal Alexander St John McDonnell"  in Sara Foster, Zoe Gullen, eds., ''Debrett's People of Today'' (London: Debrett's, 2002), p. 568</ref>
In November 2021, Antrim, [[Edwin Poots]], and local children planted the first trees of 350 hectares of new native woodland on Antrim's estate at Glenarm Forest. Poots, who was then Northern Ireland's [[Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs|Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs]], announced this as Northern Ireland's first [[The Queen's Commonwealth Canopy|Queen's Commonwealth Canopy]] forest conservation project.<ref>Kathleen O'Sullivan, [https://www.agriland.co.uk/farming-news/glenarm-forest-is-nis-first-accredited-qcc-forest-conservation-project/ "Glenarm Forest is NI’s first accredited QCC forest conservation project"], agriland.co.uk, 9

'''Cymin Samawatie''' (* [[Braunschweig]], Germany) is a German musician of [[Iranian peoples|Iranian]] background. She is known as singer, composer, conductor and founder of several ensembles for [[Ethno jazz|ethno-jazz]] and experimental music in Germany. She has written compositions for a variety of ensembles – from [[chamber music]] to [[Big band|big bands]] – including commissions for the [[Morgenland Festival Osnabrück|Morgenland Festival]] [[Morgenland Festival Osnabrück|Osnabrück]] and the [[Berlin Philharmonic]]. Her work has been honoured with awards including the German World Music Award, the German Jazz Award and the Prize for Cultural Promotion.
In 2002, she founded the jazz quartet [[Cyminology]], combining contemporary jazz with [[Persian poetry]]. In 2003, she performed alongside the vocalist [[Bobby McFerrin]] at a concert in her home town Braunschweig.<ref name=":2">{{cite web|access-date=2026-03-25 |date=2008-07-01 |language=en |title=14º Festival Internacional de Jazz de Loulé |url=https://www.ccloule.com/festival-de-jazz-de-loule-2008-en}}<!-- auto-translated from German by Module:CS1 translator --></ref> In 2013, Samawatie along with musicians Ketan Bhatti and Philip Geisler founded the Diwan der Kontinente orchestra, later renamed [[Trickster Orchestra]]. This ensemble comprises instrumentalists from the [[Berlin Philharmonic]] and musicians from [[Syria]], [[Japan]], [[China]], [[Israel]], [[Lebanon]], [[Siberia]] and [[Turkey]]. The ensemble aims to translate the concept of the big band into contemporary and hybrid, post-migrant forms characterised by [[cultural diversity]] and the breaking down of identities.<ref name=":0">{{cite web|access-date=2026-03-25 |date=2025-06-25 |format=PDF |language=de |title=Cymin Samawatie & Ketan Bhatti erhalten den Friedlieb Ferdinand Runge-Preis für unkonventionelle Kunstvermittlung 2025 |url=https://berlinischeg

On 5 February 2025, the former residence of [[Sheikh Mujibur Rahman]], commonly known as [[Bangabandhu Memorial Museum|Dhanmondi 32]], was occupied and demolished by the protesters in [[Dhaka, Bangladesh]]. The building, which housed [[Bangabandhu Memorial Museum|a museum]] and was considered a historic site by the [[Awami League]] supporters, was torn down by protesters during a period of intense political turmoil following months after the [[resignation of Sheikh Hasina|resignation]] of the [[Prime Minister of Bangladesh|Prime Minister]] [[Sheikh Hasina]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dhakatribune.com/bangladesh/dhaka/372963/chronology-of-dhanmondi-32-destruction|title=Timeline: The demolition of Sheikh Mujib's Dhanmondi 32 residence|author=Tribune desk|date=6 February 2025|website=[[Dhaka Tribune]]|access-date=6 February 2025}}</ref>
The demolition followed an online press conference attended by Sheikh Hasina while in [[Student-People's uprising|self-imposed exile]] in [[India]], during which she addressed topics that protesters viewed as against national interests. This event occurred amid years of growing discontent regarding the political influence of the [[Tungipara Sheikh family|Sheikh family]] in the country's political landscape, and the rise of a [[Sheikh Mujibur Rahman's cult of personality|cult of personality of Sheikh Mujibur Rahman]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thedailystar.net/news/bangladesh/news/pictures-demolition-dhanmondi-32-residence-3817416|title=In pictures: Demolition of Dhanmondi-32 residence|author=Star Digital Report|date=6 February 2025|website=[[The Daily Star (Bangladesh)|The Daily Star]]|access-date=6 February 2025}}</ref>
Despite these events, the structure remained standing until the subsequent demolition in February 2025. title: Ixcateopan (archaeological site)
diff_added:
The first humans in the state's territory were nomadic hunter-gatherers who left evidence of their existence in various caves starting about 22,000 years ago.<ref name="enchistoria">{{cite web|url=http://www.e-local.gob.mx/wb2/ELOCAL/EMM_guerrero |title=Estado de Guerrero Historia |year=2005 |work=Enciclopedia de los Municipios de México |publisher=Instituto Nacional para el Federalismo y el Desarrollo Municipal |location=Mexico |language=es |trans-title=State of Guerrero History |access-date=June 24, 2010 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120306003153/http://www.e-local.gob.mx/wb2/ELOCAL/EMM_guerrero |archive-date=March 6, 2012 }}</ref><ref name="riquezahis">{{cite web |url= http://www.guerrero.gob.mx/?P=readart&ArtOrder=ReadArt&Article=71 |title= La Riqueza Histórica de Guerrero |publisher= Government of Guerrero |location=Guerrero, Mexico |language=es |trans-title=The Historical Richness of Guerrero |access-date=June 24, 2010 }}</ref> Up until about 8,000 years ago, climatic conditions better favored human habitation than those today;<ref name="enchistoria"/> however, sedimentary human habitation happened around this time in the mountainous areas with more moisture, and better soil for agriculture. After that, settlements appeared near the coast because of fishing.<ref name="mediofisico">{{cite web|url=http://www.e-local.gob.mx/work/templates/enciclo/guerrero/medi.htm |title=Estado de Guerrero Medio Fisico |year=2005 |work=Enciclopedia de los Municipios de México |publisher=Instituto Nacional para el Federalismo y el Desarrollo Municipal |location=Mexico |language=es |trans-title=State of Guerrero Environment |access-date=June 24, 2010 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110616191605/http://www.e-local.gob.mx/work/templates/enciclo/guerrero/medi.htm |archive-date=June 16, 2011 }}</ref> At these sites, evidence of weaving, ceramics, basketry and other crafts have been found. Around this time, a grain called teocintle, or the forerunner to corn, became the staple of the diet.<ref name="enchistoria"/>
Fray Torbio de Benavente, the local evangelist, and Fray [[Bernardino de Sahagún]] wrote texts about the death and burial of Cuauhtémoc which were initially kept at the Church of San Hipólito in [[Mexico City]] but somehow wound up in the hands of the family of Salvador Rodriguez Juárez, who was the doctor of Ixcateopan in the first half of the 20th century.<ref name="turista"/>  The documents had been passed down in his family for generations. They told of how Cuauhtémoc's body had been recovered and brought to Ixcateopan and initially buried at the palace of his maternal grandparents in 1525. The documents indicated that this tomb was nearly directly under the main altar of the church. After Rodríguez Juárez showed the doc

title: Glen Echo Park, Ontario
diff_added:
'''Glen Echo Park''' was a [[Naturist resort|naturist club]] opened in 1955 by Marie and Eddie Todorowsky in [[King, Ontario]].<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20121104163537/http://pqasb.pqarchiver.com/thestar/access/498180841.html?FMT=ABS&FMTS=ABS:FT&type=current&date=Apr+08,+1997&author=Christine+Samuelian&pub=Waterloo+Region+Record&desc=Seniors+shed+shyness+along+with+clothes&pqatl=google "Seniors Shed Shyness Along With Clothes"], ''[[Kitchener Record]]'', April 8, 1997 (pay site).</ref>  When it closed in 2009, it was the oldest nudist camp in Ontario.<ref>Robyn Doolittle, [https://www.thestar.com/article/221188 "Interest sags in nude retreats"], ''[[Toronto Star]]'', June 4, 2007.</ref> The camp featured a children's swimming pool, a playground, a five-unit motel and a shower area. In 1974, a {{convert|10000|sqft|m2|adj=on}} clubhouse was constructed for complete year-round use.
The announcement that the club would close effective October 1, 2009 only gave members about a month's notice.<ref>{{cite letter|title=Letter to members|recipient=Members of Glen Echo Park |date=August 21, 2009}}</ref> The news of the sale and closure came as a surprise to the membership because they had been given repeated assurances that the park would be sold to someone who would maintain it as a nudist club.<ref>{{cite news|title=The Sale of Glen Echo Park|newspaper=The Echo|date=Spring 2005}}</ref><ref>{{cite letter|title=Letter to members|recipient=Members of Glen Echo Park|date=December 1, 2005}}</ref> Because some had significant investments in elaborate cabins that could not easily be moved, many members felt betrayed.<ref name="staraugust2009" />

{{Nihongo|'''''Corpse Party'''''|コープスパーティー|'''''Kōpusu Pātī'''''|lead=yes}} is a [[Horror game|horror video game]] originally created by Makoto Kedōin<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.memoriesoffear.com/games_corpsepartypc98/index.html|title=Memories of Fear...''Corpe Party'' Rebuilt|website=memoriesoffear.com|access-date=2014-10-17|archive-date=2014-07-06|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140706100042/http://www.memoriesoffear.com/games_corpsepartypc98/index.html|url-status=dead}}</ref> and eventually developed by Team GrisGris.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.gris2.com/gris_product.htm|title=Products|publisher=Team GrisGris|access-date=24 October 2015|language=ja|archive-date=4 March 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304063317/http://www.gris2.com/gris_product.htm|url-status=live}}</ref> Blending elements and genres of [[Supernatural|supernatural horror]], [[survival horror]], [[Adventure game|adventure]], and [[doujin soft]], the video game often features high school students who attempt to escape after becoming trapped in an abandoned, parallel school, haunted by a vengeful spirit in a red dress.
The video game has spawned several [[Manga|mangas]], an [[anime]] [[OVA]], [[Drama CD|drama CDs]], [[Sunshine City, Tokyo#Namco Namja Town|theme park]] attractions, and two [[Corpse Party (film)|live action films]].
==History==
The first game in the franchise was developed using the [[RPG Maker]] [[game development tool|software]] version RPG Tsukūru Dante 98 and released on April 22, 1996, for the [[PC-9801]]. It was followed by three remakes:
#''Corpse Party: Blood Covered... Repeated Fear'',{{efn|name=''RepeatedFear''}} which was released for the [[PlayStation Portable]] on August 12, 2010, and [[iOS]] on February 9, 2012. It was released on PC Steam in 2021, under the title ''Corpse Party (2021)''.
The game was released in North America and Europe by [[Xseed Games]] and [[Marvelous (company)|Marvelous Europe]] under the title ''Corpse Party''.<ref name="Anime News Network">{{cite web|url=https://www.animenewsnetwork.com/interest/2011-09-02/xseed-licenses-corpse-party-horror-psp-game|first=Jennifer|last=Sherman|title=Marvelous USA Licenses ''Corpse Party'' Horror PSP Game|date=September 3, 2011|access-date=September 3, 2011|website=[[Anime News Network]]|archive-date=August 26, 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210826115631/https://www.animenewsnetwork.com/interest/2011-09-02/xseed-licenses-corpse-party-horror-psp-game|url-status=live}}</ref> [[Xseed Games]] planned to release a localized version of ''Corpse Party: Blood Covered'' for PC in North America in 2015,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.siliconera.com/2014/06/05/xseed-bringing-corpse-party-pc-2014/|title=Xseed Bringing ''Corpse Party'' To PC In 2014|website=Siliconera|date=June 5, 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608003236/https://www.siliconera.com/2014/06/05/xseed-bringing-corpse-party-pc-2014/|archive-

title: Sacramento Regional Wastewater Treatment Plant
diff_added:
The Central Valley Regional Water Control Board is requiring the project to meet discharge requirements of all treatment plants in the state by 2023, hence the EchoWater Project. According to ''Industry Tap'' the project name comes from the idea that "just like sound waves echo back to their original source," so too will SRWTP's water entering the Sacramento River.<ref>{{cite web |url = http://www.industrytap.com/wastewater-treatment-plant-upgrade-sacramento/28191 |title = Wastewater Treatment Plant Upgrade for Sacramento |last1 = Schilling |first1 = David |website = Industry Tap }}</ref> The EchoWater Project construction started on May 28, 2015, and although required completion date is by 2023 SRWTP is hoping to achieve this before then and is on track with a 2021 finish. Upon the completion of the project, discharged ammonia levels should be reduced approximately 95%, therefore increasing the water quality, the ability to recycle water, and survival rates of the nearly extinct Delta Smelt.<ref name=":5" /> Ammonia, nitrates, and other pathogens will be removed through the addition of hypochlorite, sodium bisulphite, and filtering and disinfection layers that will improve the discharged water to a tertiary status that can then be used for landscaping, agriculture, and power plants.<ref name=":4" /><ref name=":1" /> [[Biological nutrient removal|Biological Nutrient Removal]] (BNR) and a Return Activated Sludge systems are other new additions that will also enhance water quality and have a price tag of approximately $500 M.<ref name=":4" />
The EchoWater Projects consists of 12 projects that are primarily funded by California's Clean Water State Revolving Fund that contributed $1.6 B and the State Water Resources Control Board. Monetary aide from these two entities will save Sacramento area customers from high interest costs to their monthly bill statements. When the project first began in 2010, monthly bills were projected to increase to at least $60 per month, now that number has drastically shrunk to only $39 per month in 2021.<ref name=":6" /> EchoWater is the largest project in the history of Sacramento, even surpassing the Sacramento International Airport and new Golden 1 Center Arena in cost.
SRWTP is one of the state's main contributors of [[biosolids]], also known as treated effluent, and processes a staggering 26,000 dry tons every year. This recycled waste goes into everyday products and is used for soil enhancement. Wastewater is high in N, K, P, and other nutrients which makes it a successful fertilizer, and it also aides in moisture retention and reduces erosion. '''''Sunshine By My Side''''' is a 2023 Chinese urban romance TV series produced by [[China Central Television|CCTV]], [[iQiyi]], [[Tencent Video]] and [[New Classics Media]]. It is directed by Song Xiaofei, written by Li Xiao and Jiang Wuji, and starred by [[Xiao Zhan]], [[Bai Baihe]], Tian Yu, and Zhu Zhu, with Yuan Wenkang, Liu Xun, Xiang Hanzhi, Wu Xingjian, and Han Qiuchi as the main actors, Yang Haoyu, Wang Lin, and Dai Lele as special actors, and Wu Dairong and Bu Guanjin as guest actors. title: Diwas
comment: /* Science behind panpipes */ clean up, [[WP:AWB/T|typo(s) fixed]]: Therefore → Therefore,
diff_added: How the instrument produces its sound can be explained by the concept of [[Acoustic resonance#Closed|closed-end pipes/tubes]]. When the player blows on the embouchure, air jets enter the body of the instrument and create vibrations. The slanted cut in the embouchure controls the airflow in the body. The time for this vibration to reach the end of the body and get reflected back determines the frequency produced by the instrument. Therefore, the length of the tube determines the frequency produced by the instrument.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Panpipes |url=http://www.sarahtulga.com/panpipes.htm |website=sarahtulga.com |language=en}}</ref>
