title: The White Ribbon
diff_added:
The puritanical pastor leads confirmation classes and gives his pubescent children a guilty conscience over apparently small transgressions. He has them wear white ribbons as a reminder of the innocence and purity from which they have strayed. When under interrogation, his son confesses to 'impure' touching;<!-- For those editors who would like to explicitly state that the son was accused of masturbation, please note that the film doesn't. The discussion on the subject led to a consensus that the plot summary remain consistent with the action of the film. --> the pastor has the boy's hands tied to his bed frame each night. The doctor, a widower, treats the village children kindly but humiliates his housekeeper (the local [[midwife]], with whom he is having sexual relations) and also sexually abuses his teenage daughter. The baron, who is the [[lord of the manor]], underwrites harvest festivities for the villagers, many of them his farm workers.
The film received its premiere on 21 May 2009 as an official selection at the [[2009 Cannes Film Festival|62nd Cannes Film Festival]] and had its theatrical release in Austria on 25 September 2009.<ref name="derstandard" /><ref name="festival-cannes.com">{{cite web |url=http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/archives/ficheFilm/id/10900955/year/2009.html |title=Festival de Cannes: The White Ribbon |access-date=9 May 2009|work=festival-cannes.com|publisher=Cannes Film Festival|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090704231322/http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/archives/ficheFilm/id/10900955/year/2009.html|archive-date=4 July 2009}}</ref> In Germany, a release in selected cinemas on 17 September was followed by wide release on 15 October.<ref>{{Cite web |title=DAS WEISSE BAND - EINE DEUTSCHE KINDERGESCHICHTE |url=http://www.x-verleih.de/x-verleih/kino.jsp?movieid=76 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090527080056/http://www.x-verleih.de/x-verleih/kino.jsp?movieid=76 |archive-date=2009-05-27 |access-date=2026-03-08 |website=X Verleih}}</ref> American distribution by [[Sony Pictures Classics]] began 30 December 2009.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McClintock |first=Pamela |date=2009-06-18 |title=Sony to unfurl 'Ribbon' at Christmas |url=https://variety.com/2009/film/markets-festivals/sony-to-unfurl-ribbon-at-christmas-1118005099/ |access-date=2026-03-08 |website=Variety |language=en-US}}</ref>
The film was a nominee in the category [[Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film|Best Foreign Language Film]] at the [[82nd Academy Awards]]. Its submission as an [[List of submissions to the 82nd Academy Awards for Best Foreign Language Film|entry of Germany]] rather than Austria was the source of some controversy,<ref>Meza, Ed (26 August 2009). "[https://variety.com/2009/biz/markets-festivals/oscar-could-wear-white-1118007754/ Oscar could wear 'White']". ''[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]''. Retrieved 28 August 2009.</ref> since the academy would have accepted it as a submission from either country. Martin Schweighofer,

Laing fathered six sons and four daughters by four women. After his rise as a celebrity, Laing left his first wife Anne Hearne, a former nursing student (m. 1952–1966), and their five children; his fourth daughter Natascha died in 2018 of cancer, aged 48. Subsequently, he married German graphic designer Jutta Werner (m. 1974–1986) with whom he fathered three children. His ninth child, Benjamin, with German therapist Sue Sünkel, was born in 1984. title: Kaghan Valley
comment: Restored one (Revision as of 16:55, 22 October 2025) I don't know merit of this removal but it was removed in last edit with an edit summary, so I restored it in good standing.
diff_removed:
== Associated Folklores ==
=== Folk tale of Lalazar ===
The folk tale of Lala Afghani and Sohni (Lalazar) is intricately linked to the romantic saga of an Afghan youth named Lala and a local maiden named ''Sohni''. Rooted in the cultural fabric of the region, this narrative unfolds as Lala, while passing through the village, becomes enamored with Sohni. However, societal disapproval prevails, leading to the tragic demise of the young man at the hands of the local community.

The site of Lala's sacrifice has since gained renown as Lalazar, with "Zar" signifying the location where Lala made the ultimate sacrifice for his love. Notably, the adjacent forest is christened Sohni da Banr, translating to the forest of ''Sohni''.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last1=Arif |first1=Anas Mahmud |last2=Hameed |first2=Abdul |last3=Fizza |first3=Syeda |date=2021-09-30 |title=Cultural Tourism in Kaghan Valley: Potential and Problems |journal=Pakistan Journal of Social Research |volume=3 |issue=3 |pages=23–43 |doi=10.52567/pjsr.v3i3.223 |issn=2710-3129|doi-access=free }}</ref>

In contemporary times, Lalazar has evolved into a well-known village, emerging as a burgeoning tourist destination within the valley. Simultaneously, Sohni da Banr retains its prominence as a distinguished forest in the area, preserving the essence of the timeless tale of Lala Afghani and Sohni. The rich cultural heritage associated with Lalazar continues to attract visitors, offering a glimpse into the enduring allure of this captivating narrative.<ref name=":0" />

=== Folk tale of Munshi and Darshi (Paras) ===
The romantic saga of Munshi and Darshi (Paras) unfolds against the backdrop of a renowned village called Paras in the Kaghan Valley.<ref name=":0" /> During the British colonial era, Munshi, employed as a worker for a company, toiled in the local forest. It was here that he became entangled in a love affair with Darshi, a girl from the community. The clandestine meetings of Munshi and Darshi became the talk of the town, with their love story gaining prominence in the region.

However, societal resistance prevailed, and Munshi faced rejection from the locals, ultimately meeting a tragic end at their hands. The site of this fateful event garnered fame as Darshi da Banr, signifying the forest of Darshi. This woodland has since become a popular destination within the valley, celebrated for its breathtaking beauty, diverse wildlife, and suitability for hiking adventures.
llm_reason: The edit abruptly ends mid-sentence and removes content without clear justification. The editor's comment suggests uncertainty about the removal's merit, raising suspicion.

title: Economy of Florida
diff_added:
Florida's economy ranks among the largest in the country. As of 2024, the [[gross state product]] (GSP) is about $1.647{{spaces}}trillion, the fourth largest economy in the United States. According to a June 3, 2025 forecast by Florida TaxWatch, Florida's economy reached nearly US$1.5 trillion in gross state product and is projected to continue growing through 2030, although at a gradually slower rate.<ref name="GDPByState" /> Florida is responsible for 5.82% of the United States' approximately $28{{spaces}}trillion gross domestic product (GDP).<ref name="GDPByState" /> {{as of|2024}}, Florida's nominal GDP is larger than all but 15 countries; if it were a sovereign nation, it would be ranked ahead of [[Spain]] and behind [[South Korea]].<ref name="GDPByState" /><ref name="GDP IMF" /> In terms of [[Purchasing Power Parity]], it is larger than all but 22 countries.<ref>{{cite web |title=GDP, PPP (current international $)—Data |url=https://data.worldbank.org/indicator/NY.GDP.MKTP.PP.CD |website=data.worldbank.org}}</ref> Agriculture, tourism, industry, construction, international banking, biomedical and life sciences, healthcare research, simulation training, aerospace and defense, and commercial space travel contribute to the state's economy.
The top five industries in Florida for employment are: trade, transportation, and utilities; government; professional and business services; education and health services; and leisure and hospitality.<ref>{{cite web |date=September 2018 |title=Florida's September Employment Figures Released |url=http://lmsresources.labormarketinfo.com/library/press/release.pdf |access-date=October 23, 2018 |website=Lmsresources.labormarketinfo.com}}</ref> Forecasts indicate that Florida will continue experiencing rapid population growth over the next several years, which is expected to drive long-term employment expansion in multiple industries.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Data On Demand {{!}} B.E.B.R. - Bureau of Economic and Business Research |url=https://bebr.ufl.edu/data-on-demand/ |access-date=2025-11-24 |language=en-US}}</ref> Data shows that job gains are likely to be strongest in healthcare, education, construction, and service-based fields (University of Florida Bureau of Economic and Business Research, 2023). In output, the five largest sectors are: finance, insurance, real estate, rental, and leasing, followed by professional and business services; government and government enterprises; educational services, health care, and social assistance; and retail trade.<ref>{{Cite web |date=October 23, 2018 |title=Florida |url=https://apps.bea.gov/regional/bearfacts/pdf.cfm?fips=12000&areatype=STATE&geotype=3 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181023034758/https://apps.bea.gov/regional/bearfacts/pdf.cfm?fips=12000&areatype=STATE&geotype=3 |archive-date=October 23, 2018}}</ref>

title: Former Residence of Hu Yaobang
diff_added:
The '''Former Residence of Hu Yaobang''' or '''Hu Yaobang's Former Residence''' ({{lang-zh|s=胡耀邦故居|t=胡耀邦故居|p=Hú Yàobāng Gùjū}}) was built during the Xianfeng period of the Qing Dynasty (1851–1862) and is located in Cangfang Village, [[Zhonghe, Liuyang|Zhonghe Town]], [[Liuyang]], [[Hunan]].{{sfnb|Wang Xijia|2014|p=19}} It covers a building area of about {{convert|450|m2}} including buildings such as the old houses, Yaobang Square, the ancestral temple, the ancestral grave, the Cultural Relic Exhibition Hall, and the old well.{{sfnb|Wang Xijia|2014|p=19}}<ref>{{cite web |url=http://travel.sina.com.cn/china/2013-07-15/1044199964.shtml |script-title=zh:红色旅游 长沙市浏阳胡耀邦故里旅游区 |date=2013-07-15|publisher=|work=sina|language=zh}}</ref><ref name="fhsi">{{cite web |url=http://news.qq.com/a/20130507/000156.htm?pgv_ref=aio2012&ptlang=2052 |script-title=zh:胡耀邦故居成全国重点文保单位 去年获批4A景区|date=2013-05-07|publisher=|work=Tencent|language=zh}}</ref><ref name="time">{{cite web |url=http://travel.sohu.com/20140414/n398197691.shtml?pvid=6aa0c692c0b968f8 |script-title=zh:走进湖南胡耀邦故居 |date=14 April 2014 |publisher=|work=sohu|language=zh}}</ref><ref name="bee">{{cite web |url=http://politics.people.com.cn/n/2013/0506/c1001-21375524.html |script-title=zh:胡耀邦故居列入第七批全国重点文物保护单位|date=2013-05-06|publisher=|work=people.com.cn|language=zh}}</ref><ref name="tiger">{{cite web|url=http://money.163.com/14/0414/11/9PPO0T5S00253B0H.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140821154333/http://money.163.com/14/0414/11/9PPO0T5S00253B0H.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=21 August 2014|script-title=zh:港媒：胡锦涛到访胡耀邦故居 向其铜像鞠躬致敬|date=14 April 2014|publisher=|work=163.COM|language=zh}}</ref>
The residence was built by [[Hu Yaobang]]'s great-grandfather Hu Mingzhong ({{lang-zh|s=|p=|labels=no|c=胡明鍾|t=}}) and Hu Mingzhong's elder brother Hu Mingjing ({{lang-zh|s=|p=|labels=no|c=胡明鏡|t=}}) in the [[Qing dynasty]] during the reign of the [[Xianfeng Emperor]] (1851–1862).
In 1962, when Hu Yaobang returned home and visited it, he told his relatives not to rebuild the home. Despite this wish, the residence was rebuilt in February 1995 by the People's Government of Liuyang City. In September, Hu Yaobang's wife Li Zhao ({{lang-zh|s=李昭|p=|labels=no}}) visited the residence. The next year, the residence was listed as a Hunan Province most important culture and relics site.
In 2002, it was listed as a Provincial Patriotic Education Base. In February 2005, the Cultural Relic Exhibition Hall was built. On 6 May 2013, it was listed as a "[[List of Major National Historical and Cultural Sites in Hunan|Major National Historical and Cultural Sites]]" by the [[State Council of the People's Republic of China|State Council of China]].{{sfnb|Wang Xijia|2014|p=19}} On 12 April 2014, [[Hu Jintao]] visited the residence.<ref>{{cite news |author1=Zhang Wenzhong ({{lang-zh|s=张文中|labels=no}}) |script-title=zh:胡锦涛参观胡耀邦故居有什么政治含意？ |url=http://www.rfi.fr/cn/%E4%B8%AD%E5%9B%BD/20140417-%E8%83%A1%E9%94%A6%E6%B6%9B%E5%8F%82%E8%A

title: 2024 Worthing Borough Council election
diff_added:
The '''2024 Worthing Borough Council election''' was held on Thursday 2 May 2024, alongside the [[2024 UK local elections|other local elections being held in the United Kingdom]] on the same day.<ref>{{cite news |last=Courea |first=Eleni  |date=2024-04-05 |title=England local elections: what's up for grabs on 2 May and how do predictions look? |url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2024/apr/05/england-may-local-elections-whats-up-for-grabs-predictions |access-date=2024-04-11 |work=The Guardian |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077}}</ref>
At the [[2024 United Kingdom general election|2024 United Kingdom General Election]], Council leader [[Beccy Cooper|Dr Beccy Cooper]] was elected as the MP for [[Worthing West (UK Parliament constituency)|Worthing West]].<ref>{{Cite news |title=Worthing West - General election results 2024 |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/election/2024/uk/constituencies/E14001599 |access-date=2024-07-26 |work=BBC News |language=en-GB}}</ref> As a result, she resigned as Leader and as a Councillor on Wednesday July 10, neccecitating a by-election in Marine Ward.<ref name="adur-worthing.gov.uk">{{Cite web |title=News {{!}} PR24-055 {{!}} Dr Beccy Cooper stands down as Worthing Borough Council leader and councillor - Adur & Worthing Councils |url=https://www.adur-worthing.gov.uk/news/pr24-055.html |access-date=2024-07-26 |website=www.adur-worthing.gov.uk |language=en}}</ref> Cllr Sophie Cox replaced Cooper as Leader on Tuesday 23 July 2024.<ref name="adur-worthing.gov.uk"/>
The Liberal Democrats went into the election defending their last seat on the council, in Tarring ward, having been in overall control of the council as recently as [[2003 Worthing Borough Council election|2003]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2003-05-02 |title=Worthing Borough Council: No overall control |url=https://www.theargus.co.uk/news/5112008.worthing-borough-council-no-overall-control/ |access-date=2024-04-11 |website=The Argus |language=en}}</ref>

The '''Sardar-e-Jung''' (Leader of Battle) was the second highest military decoration  by the awarded by the [[Azad Hind Government]]. First instituted by [[Subhas Chandra Bose]] in Germany, it was later also awarded to troops of the [[Indian National Army]] in [[South East Asia]]. The award could be conferred with swords for valour in combat, and without swords for non-combat awards. At least five awards were made, one to Colonel [[Shaukat Hayat Malik]] for the capture of [[Moirang]], to Captain [[Shangara Singh Mann]] and to Lieutenant Kunwar Balwant Singh for capturing British Army Post at [[Modak]]. Captain Mann was also awarded the [[Vir-e-Hind]] medal. Colonel Pritam Singh was awarded Sardar-e-jung, he captured a hill named Pritam Hill in Myanmar, Burma & Palel Airport, Major [[Pitri Sharan Raturi]] was awarded for Battle of Kaladan.

title: Fritzi Ritz
diff_added:
Distributed by [[United Feature Syndicate]], ''Fritzi Ritz'' began October 9, 1922, in the ''[[New York World|New York Evening World]]''.  Whittington left after three years, and starting May 14, 1925,<ref name=Holtz/> 20-year-old Bushmiller stepped in as his replacement, eventually modeling Fritzi after his fiancée, Abby Bohnet, whom he married in 1930. In 1931, when the ''Evening World'' and the ''[[New York World-Telegram|New York Telegram]]'' merged, ''Fritzi Ritz'' was briefly without a paper in New York City until it was picked up on January 10, 1932, by the ''[[New York Daily Mirror]]''.<ref name=toon>{{Cite web|url=http://www.toonopedia.com/fritzi.htm|title=Don Markstein's Toonopedia: Fritzi Ritz|website=www.toonopedia.com}}</ref>
Bushmiller's dailies during the mid-to-late 1930s and early 1940s involved a lot of continuity, as opposed to the [[gag-a-day]] format that would come to dominate the rest of Bushmiller's run.  There is a lot of sight humor but the strip is much wordier with elements involving mystery, pathos, and melodramatic aspects that are almost completely missing from the current version, and which were almost non-existent during the 1950s-70s strips.
Bushmiller ceased drawing the Sundays in the late 1940s.<ref name=lam/> Later ''Fritzi'' Sundays were drawn by various ghost artists, such as Bernard "Dib" Dibble<ref>{{Cite web | url=https://www.lambiek.net/artists/d/dibble_bernard.htm |title = Bernard Dibble}}</ref> and [[Al Plastino]], who would eventually officially take over the ''Nancy'' Sundays following the death of Bushmiller.<ref>{{cite web| url = https://www.lambiek.net/artists/p/plastino_al.htm| title = Al Plastino - Lambiek Comiclopedia}}</ref>

title: West Perry School District
diff_added: On July 1, 1964, three Perry County school systems (Green Park Union, Perry Joint, and Blain Union) consolidated into the West Perry School District. [[West Perry High School]], located in [[Green Park, Pennsylvania]], had served as Green Park Union High School. Green and white were selected as West Perry's colors because at the time of the consolidation, Green Park Union (whose colors were green and white) had just purchased new band uniforms and the district did not want to replace them. The current West Perry Middle School opened in 1967 as Green Park Elementary; a transfer of functions with the old middle school (now New Bloomfield Elementary) took place with the 2001–2002 academic year.

title: Agricultural Involution
comment: link "involution (sociology)" links back to the article itself, so changing link to involution disambiguation page instead
diff_added: '''''Agricultural Involution: The Processes of Ecological Change in Indonesia''''' is one of the most famous of the early works of [[Clifford Geertz]]. Its principal thesis is that many centuries of intensifying wet-rice cultivation in Indonesia had produced greater social complexity without significant technological or political change, a process Geertz terms—"[[involution|involution]]". The term, also known as [[Neijuan]], has drawn significant attention in China since its introduction in China's social sciences research, making it one of the most popular buzzwords in China.<ref>{{Cite magazine |date=2021-05-14 |title=China's "Involuted" Generation |url=https://www.newyorker.com/culture/cultural-comment/chinas-involuted-generation |access-date=2022-05-14 |magazine=The New Yorker |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite journal |author=刘世定; 邱泽奇 |year=2004 |title="内卷化"概念辨析 |url=http://www.cnki.com.cn/Article/CJFDTotal-SHXJ200405008.htm |journal=社会学研究 |volume= |issue=5 |page= |doi= |pmid= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190504230754/http://www.cnki.com.cn/Article/CJFDTOTAL-SHXJ200405008.htm |archive-date=2019-05-04 |access-date=2019-05-12}}</ref>

diff_added: In May 2025, Nvidia discontinued developer support for the Maxwell, Pascal, and Volta architectures, which includes the Maxwell-based models of the GTX 800M Series.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-toolkit-release-notes/index.html |title=NVIDIA CUDA Toolkit Release Notes |access-date=2025-06-03}}</ref> On July 1, 2025, Nvidia announced that driver branch 580 will be the last to support these architectures.<ref>{{Cite web |title=NVIDIA confirms end of support for GeForce GTX 700, 900 and 10 series after 580 drivers |url=https://videocardz.com/newz/nvidias-next-major-gpu-driver-branch-to-drop-support-for-geforce-gtx-700-900-and-10-series |access-date=2025-07-23 |website=VideoCardz.com |language=en-US}}</ref>

title: 2015 Israel State Cup final

title: Rekik Bekele
diff_added:
Rekik Bekele was born and raised in [[Addis Ababa]], [[Ethiopia]], and she is also the founder of Green Scene Energy PLC.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-10-18 |title=Rekik Bekele: Powering Change Through Renewable Energy In Ethiopia |url=https://empiremagazineafrica.com/rekik-bekele-powering-change-through-renewable-energy-in-ethiopia/ |access-date=2026-03-26 |language=en-US}}</ref>
